title: Adler site registry
description: Schema for adler-core/data/sites.json. Field-level validation for editors and CI; deeper invariants (CSS-selector validity, case-insensitive duplicate names) are enforced by the Rust loader.

_comment.description: Free-text provenance/attribution note. Ignored by the loader.

engines.description: Shared detection-signature templates referenced by `site.engine`. Forum-software platforms (Discourse, vBulletin, XenForo, …) host thousands of instances with identical detection rules; an engine defines those rules once. Site fields override engine fields on conflict.

name.description: Human-readable site name. Doubles as the case-insensitive --only/--exclude filter key; must be unique. Restricted character class (word chars plus space and a few punctuation marks) keeps the name safe to interpolate into shell, CSV, and CLI arguments — schema gate against injection via PR-supplied site entries.

url.description: URL template. Must start with http(s):// and contain the {username} placeholder. Prefer a stable API/feed endpoint over a bot-protected canonical page (see CONTRIBUTING.md).

signals.description: Detection signals. Aggregation is negative-priority: any NotFound vote wins over Found; no votes -> Uncertain.

known_present.description: One or more usernames known to exist on this site. Accepts either a single string (compact, legacy) or a non-empty array (defensive — `adler --doctor` passes the present-check if any element resolves to Found, guarding against sites that special-case specific accounts).

known_absent.description: A username known not to exist. Optional; the doctor generates a random nonsense username when omitted.

extract.description: Optional CSS-selector rules for --enrich profile extraction.

tags.description: Free-form classification tags for --tag subset scans (e.g. "social", "dev", "region:ru"). Conventionally lowercase.

request_headers.description: Extra HTTP headers sent with the probe (e.g. `X-IG-App-ID` for Instagram). Browser backends apply via setExtraHTTPHeaders/setUserAgentOverride; the raw-HTTP path doesn't read this yet.

regex_check.description: Username-validity regex (Rust `regex` syntax). Usernames not matching this pattern are skipped with `Uncertain(UsernameNotAllowed)` — no HTTP request is issued. Mirrors Sherlock's `regexCheck`.

engine.description: Name of a shared engine to inherit signature fields from (must match a key in the top-level `engines` block). Engine fields fill in where the site's own equivalents are empty/unset; site fields override on conflict.

strip_bad_char.description: Characters the site silently strips from usernames server-side (e.g. dots on Twitter handles). Adler removes any matching characters from the probe username before URL substitution so the probe hits the canonical form.

request_method.description: HTTP method used to probe this site. Defaults to GET. POST is used by a handful of API endpoints (Anilist GraphQL, Discord, Holopin) that require the username in the request body.

request_body.description: Request body to send when `request_method` is POST. The literal `{username}` is substituted with the probe username. Sent with `Content-Type: application/json` by default; sites needing a different type declare it via `request_headers`.

protection.description: Specific anti-bot mechanisms the site is known to deploy. Sites with any entry here route through the browser backend even without the legacy `bot-protected` tag. Future router logic can pick a backend per-mechanism (cloudscraper vs. full browser vs. skip).

disabled.description: Park the site without removing it: disabled entries are skipped by every filter, never probed, never appear in `--list-sites`. Use for known-broken signatures so a future contributor can re-enable by flipping the flag once a working signature exists.

disabled_reason.description: Free-form annotation explaining why this entry is disabled. Conventions in use: `duplicate of <canonical>` for importer dedup, `Honest Limits: …` for structurally unscrapable sites, `doctor: 3+ consecutive structural failures` for nightly-aggregator parking. Only meaningful when `disabled` is also true.

source.description: Canonical-site name when this entry is a mirror. Lets UX surface 'Twitter is offline, here's the same account on Nitter' without hand-curated linkage.

popularity.description: Curated popularity rank (lower = more popular). `adler --top N` treats N as a rank ceiling (`popularity <= N`), then sorts by rank. Sites without a rank are excluded from `--top` queries.

engine.description: Inheritable signature template for a forum/blog/wiki software family. Carries the same kinds of fields a site does, minus the per-site URL.
